remaining_failures.query: What was the most recent architecture change?
remaining_failures.root_cause: Memory 59 (architecture mention) outranks memory 46 (architecture change) — both contain 'architecture' but only 46 is about a CHANGE. Embedding similarity cannot distinguish event vs mention.
remaining_failures.fix: Nomic-embed upgrade (+9.1 MTEB) would produce more discriminative embeddings that separate 'architecture change' from 'architecture discussion'

remaining_failures.query: Why did we change the database AND what was the impact on the timeline?
remaining_failures.root_cause: Compound query embedded as single vector dilutes both sub-topics. Memory 16 (discusses databases peripherally) outranks memory 18 (the actual database change).
remaining_failures.fix: Multi-query embedding fusion: decompose into sub-queries, embed each, average embeddings, search with averaged vector. Existing decompose_query() handles sub-queries but only for RRF — not for the embedding itself.

remaining_failures.query: What patterns do you see in our decision-making?
remaining_failures.root_cause: Abstract meta-reasoning query. 'Patterns' has no direct BM25/entity match to any memory. Memory 52 (a specific decision) matches via entity overlap but ranks at 2.
remaining_failures.fix: Query-time synonym/concept expansion: 'patterns in decision-making' → expand with 'decisions', 'choices', 'trade-offs', 'rationale'. Feed expanded terms to BM25.

mode_1_parameter_expansion.evidence: 20 signals with <2% individual contribution each. Adding signal #21 would contribute <1%. The 20-signal ensemble is already well-saturated. Every proposed orthogonal signal (causal, centrality, novelty, completeness, coherence) was rejected as non-orthogonal.
mode_1_parameter_expansion.ceiling: MRR +0.005 max from any new signal

mode_2_tuning.evidence: Cross-encoder 12%→18%, contradiction 0.15→0.20, access intent-dependent — all applied. Temporal superlative resolution applied. Remaining tuning: possibly temporal 'this-week' penalty and ontological rerank budget. But tuning the same signals against 20 fixed benchmark queries risks overfitting.
mode_2_tuning.ceiling: MRR +0.010 max from tuning, with overfitting risk

mode_3_multiplicative_levers.evidence: Three multiplicative improvements that lift ALL 20 signals simultaneously:

levers.name: Nomic-embed model upgrade
levers.orthogonality: TRUE — changes the foundation all signals operate on

levers.name: Multi-query embedding fusion
levers.orthogonality: TRUE — changes how queries are represented, not how memories are scored

levers.name: BM25 concept expansion
levers.orthogonality: TRUE — expands query terms, not memory scores

cluster_1_query_representation.description: ALL 20 signals and ALL tuning operate on a SINGLE query embedding generated at recall.rs:231. If that embedding is poor, no amount of scoring refinement helps. Multi-query fusion and concept expansion both operate on this pre-retrieval step.
cluster_1_query_representation.coverage_in_evaluations: ZERO — every Supernova and Bifocal examined the scoring pipeline, not the query pipeline

cluster_2_embedding_foundation.description: MiniLM-L6-v2 is a 384-dim model trained on general web text. Memory content is conversational/technical. The domain mismatch means vectors are ~70% as discriminative as they could be.
cluster_2_embedding_foundation.coverage_in_evaluations: ZERO — assumed as fixed infrastructure in every evaluation

cluster_3_benchmark_saturation.description: 20 queries, each analyzed 10+ times across evaluations. We know exactly which 3 fail and why. Further tuning against these 3 queries risks overfitting. Need benchmark expansion to calibrate.
cluster_3_benchmark_saturation.coverage_in_evaluations: Mentioned in D3 unseen dimensions but not acted on

verdict.next_action: Multi-query embedding fusion (4-6 hours, query-side fix)
verdict.v0.7.0_milestone: Nomic-embed upgrade (3-5 days, biggest single lever)
verdict.stop_tuning: Do NOT further tune Layer 5 weights against LOCOMO-20. Expand benchmark first.
